input.title: E-Commerce Platform
input.description: This document describes the Software Requirements Specification (SRS) for an E-Commerce platform, a web-based shopping platform for buying and selling products online

user_classes.Customer: End-users who interact with the product catalog, shopping cart, and checkout.
user_classes.Admin: Users with the ability to manage products, orders, and users.

definitions.Customer: A user who browses and purchases products.
definitions.Admin: A user with administrative privileges for managing the platform.

constraints: No mobile application development is included in the scope
Integration with third-party marketplaces and cryptocurrency payment support is out of scope

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based application accessible via modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari).
operating_environment.backend: Node.js with Next.js framework, Prisma ORM for database interactions, and MongoDB for data storage.

user_interface: Web-based interface with responsive design for both desktop and mobile screen sizes
hardware_interfaces: No specific hardware requirements; the system will be deployed on cloud infrastructure
software_interfaces: The backend will interact with MongoDB via Prisma ORM for data storage
communication_interfaces: The system will communicate with email servers to send confirmation emails

FR-01.title: User Login
requirements: Two input fields for email and password
The email must be validated for the correct format
The password field should have a show/hide toggle

FR-02.title: User Registration
requirements: Registration form with name, email, and password fields
Password must be at least 8 characters with one uppercase and one number
System shall send verification email after registration
Duplicate email addresses shall not be allowed

FR-03.title: Product Catalog
requirements: Display all products with name, image, price, and category
Users can filter products by category and price range
Users can sort products by price, name, and newest
Pagination with 20 products per page

FR-04.title: Shopping Cart
requirements: Users can add products to cart with quantity selection
Users can update item quantities in cart
Users can remove items from cart
Cart displays subtotal, tax, and grand total
Cart persists across sessions for logged-in users

FR-05.title: Order Placement
requirements: Users can place orders from cart with delivery address
System validates stock availability before order confirmation
Order confirmation page shows order summary
System generates unique order ID

NFR-01.title: Performance
requirements: The system shall load pages in under 2 seconds
API response time shall be under 500ms

NFR-02.title: Security
requirements: All passwords must be hashed using bcrypt
SSL encryption for all client-server communication
JWT tokens for authentication with 24-hour expiry

system_attributes.reliability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time
system_attributes.scalability: The platform should be able to scale horizontally to support increased traffic during peak seasons
system_attributes.security: All passwords should be hashed and stored securely; SSL encryption must be used for all communications between the client and server
system_attributes.maintainability: The platform should have a modular architecture, making it easy to add new features or update existing ones

FR-01.title: Design User Login UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for user login. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for user login is complete and reviewable.
The user login design is responsive and accessible.
The design for user login aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of User Login
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for user login. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Two input fields for email and password; The email must be validated for the correct format; The password field should have a show/hide toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required user login user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for user login are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for user login is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of User Login
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for user login. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Two input fields for email and password; The email must be validated for the correct format; The password field should have a show/hide toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for user login is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for user login enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for user login is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Integrate User Login with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate user login with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by user login exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for user login integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by user login are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing User Login Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for user login. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for user login.
Security, validation, and permission checks for user login are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important user login behaviors.

FR-02.description: Implement the security controls required for user registration. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.

Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for user registration are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of user registration satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-02.description: Integrate user registration with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
